story: A product manager named Derek was evaluating the performance of two different marketing campaigns. The first campaign generated 150 leads from a small email list, while the second generated 120 leads from a larger list with lower engagement. Derek concluded the first campaign was clearly superior and recommended doubling its budget. He didn't account for the fact that the second campaign had a higher conversion rate among its recipients. He also failed to normalize the data by the list sizes. When a junior analyst pointed out the discrepancy, Derek said, 'You're overcomplicating it. Just look at the raw numbers—the first campaign brought in more leads. That's what matters.'
